claims.mitigation_description: Every consequential agent decision emits a signed Intent Decision Record (IDR) into an append-only forest: HMAC-SHA256 (or ed25519+ML-DSA-65) per-record signatures, predecessor linkage inside the signed body, RFC-6962 Merkle root over sorted content addresses. Any byte alteration is detectable by recomputation. Source is public; the verification is arithmetic, not attestation.

The forest Merkle root is committed to Bitcoin via OpenTimestamps, so even the record custodian cannot rewrite history undetectably. The production decision-chain root (content address 0bba0792..., 501 decisions) is confirmed in Bitcoin block 956992; any party can check the OTS proof against a public block explorer with no trust in the authors.

Every outward claim carries a receipt binding it to a verbatim quotation at recorded byte offsets in a content-addressed source; a fabricated quotation can only record as not_found and marks the deliverable un-filed-safe. An advisory model layer may only tighten, never relax, the deterministic verdict.

Verification and replay are pure, deterministic, standard-library re-derivations executable by any third party offline: re-hash, re-verify signatures, re-walk the Merkle path, re-derive the replay digest (byte-identical across machines and providers; no language model on the path). The claimant's own report is never the criterion (exogenous-anchor law). Two independent replays of an unchanged record set that disagree would falsify this claim.
